Thank you very much for reviewing this patch.

As you mentioned, there is a upstream proposals for posix_fadvise(). Would you please give me a web link to it? I tried to seach for it, but can't find it.

Thanks!

Patch Set 3:

Li, for fadvise() hints I was thinking of the topic discussed at LSF last year: http://lwn.net/Articles/548296/

However, it doesn't seem like this is moving anywhere quickly, so it is probably better just to ignore that for now.  We can always integrate the future fadvise() hints into Lustre when/if they become available.

Oleg, seems there was some confusion here. Most of your objections are against features that I asked to be added to future-proof the API and ROC protocol, knowing that the current patch doesn't support this functionality yet.

It will still be possible to improve the implementation in the future, but the RPC protocol and user API is much harder to change, which is why I asked for these changes now, before this patch goes into use anywhere.

Patch Set 35:

One question about this interface - does the client send the ladvise RPC and then immediately return to userspace, leaving the OST thread to handle the advice asynchronously, or does the client wait until the reply is sent?

It may make sense to allow the client to submit the ladvise RPCs asynchronously so that it can use this to prefetch data while it is still doing other work.  Possibly there should be a flag like LADVISE_WAIT that indicates whether the syscall will wait for the reply or not.

Patch Set 35:

That is a good idea. And currently, the reply will only sent to client after the advice is handled. I will push a new patch to enable that flag.

My main request to Li Xi would be to add a "DONTNEED" advice that drops the file data from cache, to compliment the "WILLREAD" advice that brings the data into cache. commit.subject: LU-4931 ladvise: Add feature of giving file access advices
commit.message:
LU-4931 ladvise: Add feature of giving file access advices

The fadvise() system call provided by Linux kernel enables
applications to give advices or hints about how a file will be
accessed. However, It is only a client side mechanism which is
not enough for distributed file systems like Lustre, because in
order to tune system-wide cache or read-ahead policies, servers
need to understand the advices too.

This patch adds a new feature named ladvise which provides new
APIs and utils to give advices about the access pattern of Lustre
files with the purpose of performance improvement. It is similar
to Linux fadvise() system call, except it forwards the advices
directly from Lustre client to server. The server side codes will
apply appropriate read-ahead and caching techniques for the
corresponding files.

A typical workload for ladvise is e.g. a bunch of different
clients are doing small random reads of a file, so prefetching
pages into OSS cache with big linear reads before the random IO
is a net benefit. Fetching all that data into each client cache
with fadvise() may not be, due to much more data being sent to
the client.
